revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:愛宕祭 (丹波市)]]
'''Atago Festival''' (愛宕祭, Atago Matsuri) is a traditional festival held in Narimatsu District, Hikami Town, Tamba City, Hyōgo Prefecture, since the mid-Edo period.<ref name="tamba">[http://burari.city.tamba.hyogo.jp/event/detail.php?id=1996 Tamba City Tourism Association]</ref> == Overview == Held annually on August 23rd and 24th in Narimatsu District, Tamba City, the festival is centered around {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} and has its roots in the mid-Edo period. It is also known as the "Hometown Tamba Hikami Summer Festival." The event features traditional "Tsukurimono" (craft creations) that are displayed throughout the town, performances by the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force band, and the region's largest fireworks display, including 4,000-shot {{ill|Starmine|ja|スターマイン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Starmine|1=Starmine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11312101}} fireworks, a tradition since the Taisho period. The festival attracts approximately 40,000 visitors each year.<ref name="tamba" /><ref name="jorudan">[https://sp.jorudan.co.jp/hanabi/spot_87401.html Official website of Jorudan Co., Ltd.]</ref> === Tsukurimono === "Tsukurimono" are traditional crafts created by neighborhood associations as part of the Atago Festival's rituals. Using materials such as {{ill|Metal|en|Metal|qid=Q11426|lt=metal|1=Metal}}, {{ill|ceramics|ja|陶器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陶磁器|1=Pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11642}}, celebratory items, and other everyday materials, these works reflect contemporary social trends and are displayed at dozens of locations around the town.<ref name="detail">[http://burari.city.tamba.hyogo.jp/members/detail.php?id=879 Tamba City Tourism Association - See & Play (Atago Festival Tsukurimono)]</ref> == Origins == The festival originated in the mid-Edo period when a branch shrine of {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine in Kyoto|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} was established to pray for {{ill|Bumper crop|en|Bumper crop|qid=Q4997332|lt=abundant harvests|1=Bumper crop}} and fire prevention.<ref name="jorudan" /> == Event Period == * Late August<ref name="tamba" /><ref name="jorudan" /> == Venue == Narimatsu District, Hikami Town, Tamba City, Hyōgo Prefecture.<ref name="tamba" /><ref name="jorudan" /> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Atago Matsuri|ja|愛宕祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Festival|qq=}} * {{ill|Kokuryo Minazuki Festival|ja|国領水無月祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuryo Minazuki Festival|qq=}} - A festival held in the Kokuryo District of Tamba City on July 31st. * {{ill|Kawasuso Festival|ja|川裾祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasuso Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 川裾祭}} - A festival held in Ichijima, Tamba City, on July 29th. == External Links == * [https://www.tambacity-kankou.jp/ Tamba City Tourism Association]
{{Atago Faith|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|愛宕祭 (丹波市)|version=99531224|comment=Imported full ja history}}
